title: Your Name
text:
I am an Actuarial Specialist working across cell captive arrangements, corporate actuarial valuations, and capital modelling. My valuation experience includes reporting under IFRS 17 and the Solvency Assessment and Management (SAM) framework.
I use R to support actuarial analysis, model development, and the clear communication of results. This portfolio presents selected technical work and practical perspectives for fellow actuarial and insurance professionals.

title: Research
text: Use this page to publish technical reviews, research summaries, and applied actuarial analyses. Each review can follow the structure below.

title: Research
text: Use this page to publish technical reviews, research summaries, and applied actuarial analyses. Each review can follow the structure below.

title: Research
text:
Technical Review Title

Abstract
Provide a concise summary of the question, methods, principal findings, and actuarial relevance.


Background
Introduce the problem, define the context, and cite the relevant literature.


Methodology
Describe the assumptions, data, notation, and analytical approach.
For example, the present value of a discrete future loss random variable may be written as
\[
Z = v^T b_T,
\]
where (v = (1 + i)^{-1}), (T) is the time of payment, and (b_T) is the benefit payable at time (T).
An expected present value can then be expressed as
\[
\operatorname{EPV}(Z)
= \operatorname{E}\!\left[v^T b_T\right].
\]


Results
Present the main results with clearly labelled tables, figures, and equations.


Discussion
Interpret the findings, discuss limitations, and explain their practical actuarial implications.


Conclusion
Summarize the principal insight and identify possible extensions.


References
Add references using a project bibliography or properly formatted Markdown citations.
